title: Add secure DNS interoperability and resolver-role contract surfaces
summary: Preserve the exact secure-DNS diagnostic profile while adding implementation-neutral standards-based portability lanes and bounded recursive-resolver diagnostics.
planned_changes: Add a versioned secure-DNS interoperability TLS profile and schema, valid and invalid fixtures, authoritative DoT/DoH2/DoH3/DoQ A-query scenarios, recursive-resolver DoT/DoH2 A-query scenarios with exact RD/RA wire identity and local-upstream isolation, diagnostic suites, canonical requirement and architecture updates, traceability, and repository-health validation for both profile versions.
out_of_scope: changing or weakening the exact v1 secure-DNS TLS profile or its existing scenarios
declaring any implementation conformant or adding runner, executor, package, or server behavior
public or externally managed recursive resolvers
resolver-role DoH3 or DoQ before cache and upstream control can be proven coherently
publication-grade benchmark or cross-profile ranking claims
verification_plan: Validate both secure-DNS TLS profile schemas and their positive and negative fixtures; resolve every scenario fixture, suite, and load profile; verify authoritative and recursive wire hashes and transport framing; prove resolver cache and upstream-isolation requirements are structurally present; confirm strict, interoperability, authoritative, and recursive cohorts remain distinct; and run all repository-health checks.
completion_notes: Completed on 2026-07-14 as a public-contract-only portability slice. Runtime packages and live implementation evidence remain downstream work in their owning repositories.
